items.d: To make a fun day happen and do all the work for it.
items.s: We want to ___ a surprise party for our teacher.
items.hintDe: Grundform: alles für ein Fest planen und vorbereiten.

items.d: A big fun day that friends make for you when you do not know about it before.
items.s: My friends made a ___ for me, and I did not know about it.
items.hintDe: Ein Fest, von dem die Person vorher nichts weiß.
items.note: Carrier invented (masterlist 'They planned a surprise party.' displays untaught 'planned'); SB story plot 'They planned a surprise party.' is the idea source.

items.d: The money you pay to go into a place.
items.s: The ___ to the museum is five pounds for adults.
items.hintDe: Das Geld, das du bezahlst, um hineinzukommen.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb; '5 pounds' kept (pounds taught, matches SB leaflet '£5').

items.d: Somebody who makes beautiful pictures or music.
items.s: He made this beautiful painting. He's a great ___.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der Bilder malt oder Musik macht.
items.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb.

items.d: A place where people show their art for you to look at.
items.s: There is a new art ___ at the museum.
items.hintDe: Hier kannst du Kunst anschauen, oft im Museum.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'I'm going to see the new exhibition at the museum.' ('see' untaught) — grounded by SB leaflet 'An exhibition of work from the artist'.

items.d: Not clean at all.
items.s: My shoes are very ___ because I played in the garden.
items.hintDe: So sind deine Schuhe nach dem Spielen im Garten.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'The floor was very dirty after the party.' ('floor', 'party' untaught alone).

items.d: New and from the time we live in now.
items.s: Their new school is very ___, with big windows and a garden.
items.hintDe: Neu und aus unserer heutigen Zeit.
items.note: Carrier invented with taught words; SB exampleSb 'The modern sculpture was missing.' ('missing' untaught) used as idea.

items.d: A big building where you go to look at art and beautiful pictures.
items.s: We can look at art and beautiful pictures at the ___.
items.hintDe: Ein großes Gebäude, in dem du Kunst anschauen kannst.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'She works in a museum.' ('works' untaught).

items.d: To be inside a bigger thing and not alone.
items.s: The jacket was ___ a big sculpture.
items.hintDe: Zu etwas Größerem gehören, nicht für sich allein sein.
items.note: Carrier from SB story 'Modern art' ('The jacket with the roses was part of a sculpture'); masterlist exampleSb 'The jacket was part of a sculpture.' Blank holds 'part of'; 'be/was' stays outside the blank in the sentence frame.

items.d: Art that an artist makes from stone for people to look at.
items.s: There is a big stone ___ of a horse in the park.
items.hintDe: Kunst aus Stein, um die du herumgehen kannst.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'The sculpture is huge.' plus SB-grounded idea; def avoids untaught 'walk around'/'metal'/'wood'.

items.d: To have a price, like a lot of money.
items.s: This painting is ___ a lot of money.
items.hintDe: Einen Preis haben, zum Beispiel viel Geld.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'The sculpture was worth a lot of money.' Blank holds 'worth'; 'be/is' stays in the frame.

items.d: You ask this when somebody looks sad or upset.
items.s: You look sad. ___ You look upset.
items.hintDe: Das fragst du, wenn jemand traurig aussieht.
items.note: Carrier verbatim from SB ex4 speaking prompt 'What's the matter? You look upset.' Distractors are other in-bank words (only one other bank expression exists, so noun foils complete the set).

items.d: Any man, woman, or child at all.
items.s: Don't tell your password to ___.
items.hintDe: Egal welche Person, ganz egal wer.
items.note: Carrier from SB online-safety text 'Don't give your passwords to anyone.' (masterlist 'Don't give your computer password to anyone.' has untaught 'computer'). MC are nouns; 'anyone' tests recognition by elimination.

items.d: How good or bad somebody is to people.
items.s: There is a lot of bad ___ on the web.
items.hintDe: Wie sich jemand benimmt, gut oder schlecht.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'There is a lot of wrong behaviour on the web.' ('wrong' untaught -> 'bad'; 'web' glossed, matches SB text 'wrong behaviour on the web').

items.d: To call or write to somebody.
items.s: Please ___ me if you need help.
items.hintDe: Grundform: jemanden anrufen oder anschreiben.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'Please contact us if you have any questions.' ('questions' untaught).

items.d: When a room is not clean and nothing is where it should be.
items.s: My room is a big ___. I need to clean it.
items.hintDe: Wenn alles unordentlich herumliegt.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'The garden was a mess.' to a clearer room context; 'should' glossed in d (no taught modal fits).

items.d: To give a thing or a letter to a friend.
items.s: Can you ___ this letter to your mum for me?
items.hintDe: Grundform: etwas an jemand anderen weitergeben.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'Please pass on the message to your mum.' ('message' untaught -> 'letter'). SB text uses 'don't pass it on to other people'.

items.d: A secret that you need so people cannot go into your account.
items.s: Never tell your ___ to anyone.
items.hintDe: Das geheime Wort, mit dem du dich anmeldest.
items.note: Carrier from SB online-safety text 'Don't give your passwords to anyone.' (masterlist exampleSb has untaught 'computer'/'other'). 'account' glossed in d; note 'secret' is a sibling bank word used in d but not the headword.

items.d: To put a picture or a story on the web for people to look at.
items.s: Think before you ___ a picture for everybody to look at.
items.hintDe: Grundform: etwas ins Internet stellen.
items.note: Carrier from SB online-safety text 'Think before you post something and check who can see it.' ('something' untaught -> 'a picture'). 'web' glossed in d.

items.d: A picture or a story that you put on the web for people to look at.
items.s: Many people can look at your ___, so be careful what you put online.
items.hintDe: Ein Beitrag, den du ins Internet stellst.
items.note: Carrier from SB online-safety text 'check who can see your postings'; masterlist exampleSb 'Check who can see your posting.' ('check'/'see' untaught). 'web'/'online' glossed.

items.d: You put this before 'a' to make 'a good day' into 'a very good day'.
items.s: It was ___ a good day. We were all very happy.
items.hintDe: 'so ein' — macht das nächste Wort stärker.
items.note: Carrier reworked from SB story 'Jacob isn't the only one to make such a mistake.' ('only'/'mistake' untaught) into a 'such a + adjective' frame the SB models. Definition shows usage without grammar jargon.

items.d: A good thing somebody shows you to help you do well.
items.s: Here is a good ___ to help you do well at school.
items.hintDe: Ein guter Hinweis, der dir hilft.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'Here is an important tip for when you go online.' ('important'/'online' untaught). SB text: 'here are some important tips for when you go online'.

items.d: A thing that can happen or that you can do.
items.s: I can't do all this homework. It's not ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das man machen oder schaffen kann.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'I can't finish all this homework. It's not possible.' ('finish' untaught -> 'do'). SB dialogue: 'Why is that not possible?'

items.d: Really great and amazing.
items.s: The new computer game is ___! I really like it.
items.hintDe: Richtig toll und beeindruckend.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'Sport is awesome!' ('sport' untaught -> 'computer game', an in-level phrase). 'awesome' is in the SB ex10 adjectives box.

items.d: Not fun at all. It makes you tired and you want to do something else.
items.s: This show is very ___. I don't want to watch it any more.
items.hintDe: Gar nicht spannend — du wirst müde dabei.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'This film is very boring...' ('film' untaught -> 'show', taught g1). SB ex11: 'Because it's boring.' 'something'/'else' glossed in d.

items.d: Hard to understand, so you do not know what is what.
items.s: I don't understand this exercise. It's very ___.
items.hintDe: So schwer zu verstehen, dass du durcheinanderkommst.
items.note: Carrier verbatim from masterlist exampleSb. WB ex2: 'I don't really understand it. I think it's confusing.' 'hard' glossed in d.

items.d: Not easy to do or to understand.
items.s: This homework is very ___. I can't do it.
items.hintDe: Nicht leicht zu machen oder zu verstehen.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'I don't think the text is difficult. It's easy.' ('easy' untaught in carrier -> reworded). WB ex3: 'sometimes I find it very difficult.' 'easy' glossed in d.

items.d: Making you very happy, so you can't wait for it.
items.s: Going to the zoo for the first time was really ___!
items.hintDe: Es macht dich ganz aufgeregt und froh.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'There's an exciting film on TV...' ('film'/'TV' untaught). WB ex1 lists 'exciting'.

items.d: Making you want to laugh.
items.s: The clown was very ___. We all laughed.
items.hintDe: Es bringt dich zum Lachen.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'That was funny. I laughed a lot.' reworded for an unambiguous blank. SB sketch: 'That is so funny.'

items.d: When you are not happy because you did a funny thing and people looked at you.
items.s: I was so ___ when I made a big mess in front of the class.
items.hintDe: Wenn dir etwas peinlich ist und alle hinschauen.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'I was so embarrassed. I didn't know what to say.' ('say' untaught) reworded with 'mess' (in-bank). SB sketch repeats 'I'm so embarrassed.'

items.d: A thing that you put your food on when you eat.
items.s: Put the pasta on the ___ and eat it.
items.hintDe: Darauf legst du dein Essen.
items.note: Carrier from masterlist exampleSb 'The waiter puts the plate down.' ('puts' untaught) reworded. SB sketch serves dumplings 'on a plate'.

items.d: A thing that you do not tell other people.
items.s: Don't tell anyone. It's a ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das du niemandem verrätst.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'Your secret is safe with me.' ('safe' untaught). SB sketch: 'Your secret is safe with me.' 'other' glossed in d.

items.d: When you are sad and angry because a bad thing happened.
items.s: The little girl was ___ because she had a bad day.
items.hintDe: Traurig und verärgert wegen etwas Schlimmem.
items.note: Carrier adapted from masterlist exampleSb 'The little girl looked upset because she couldn't have her favourite toy.' ('couldn't'/'favourite'/'took' untaught). SB ex4: 'You look upset.' 'little' glossed in s.

items.d: To put more to a thing that you have.
items.s: You can ___ more sugar to your tea.
items.hintDe: Grundform: noch etwas dazugeben.
items.note: Carrier adapted from v1 idea ('add sugar to tea'); masterlist exampleSb 'You should add a good title to the story.' ('should'/'title' untaught).

items.d: To not do well at school.
items.s: I studied very much because I didn't want to ___ at school.
items.hintDe: Grundform: nicht bestehen, eine schlechte Note bekommen.
items.note: verify r2 (level-gloss): removed 'mark' (school grade) from d and s — it was untaught and only passed the gate via the harvested NAME 'Mark' (proper-noun masking). Reworded to taught-only 'do well at school' / 'fail at school'; meaning (durchfallen) preserved, no gloss needed.

items.d: You tell somebody this so they know you are going to do a thing for them.
items.s: I'll help you with your homework tomorrow. ___
items.hintDe: Das sagst du, wenn du etwas fest zusagst.
items.note: Carrier from SB Everyday English ('I promise' completes the comic) + masterlist exampleSb 'I'll help you. I promise.' 'I'll' glossed in s. Distractors are other in-bank words (only one other bank expression exists).
